seoTitle: Send Automated Emails With Gmail and n8n
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Gmail to n8n with OAuth2 and automatically send personalized emails from your workflows.
intro: Automating email delivery through Gmail lets your n8n workflows send alerts, follow-ups, or reports without manual work. This guide covers connecting your Gmail account through OAuth2, customizing your message content, and using dynamic variables so each email reflects real data from earlier steps in the workflow.

steps.heading: Start a new workflow
steps.body: Create a fresh, blank automation canvas in your n8n workspace.

steps.heading: Add the Gmail node
steps.body: Search for and deploy the official Gmail node onto your canvas.

steps.heading: Authorize your Google account
steps.body: Open the credentials panel and complete the OAuth2 authentication flow to connect your target Gmail inbox.

steps.heading: Set the resource and action
steps.body: Choose Message as the resource type and Send as the action you want the node to perform.

steps.heading: Customize the email content
steps.body: Fill in the recipient address, subject line, and body text, then inject dynamic variables from earlier nodes into the message.

steps.heading: Test the delivery
steps.body: Run the workflow manually to send a test email and confirm it arrives correctly before activating continuous triggers.

faq.question: Do I need to set up a Google Cloud project to connect Gmail?
faq.answer: Yes, connecting Gmail through OAuth2 typically requires setting up API credentials through the Google Cloud console.

faq.question: Can I personalize each email with data from a previous step?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use expression syntax to pull variables from earlier nodes directly into the subject or body text.

faq.question: Is it safe to send emails from my personal Gmail account through automation?
faq.answer: It works fine for moderate volume, but be mindful of Gmail's sending limits if you're planning high-volume automated sends.

faq.question: How do I test without spamming real recipients?
faq.answer: Use a manual trigger and send to a test email address first before activating the workflow with real production triggers.
